extra.message: Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) vulnerability. User-controlled input 'name' parameter is interpolated into HTML content and passed to Jinja2's Template().render() without sanitization. An attacker can inject template syntax like '{{7*7}}' or '{{config}}' to execute arbitrary code, read sensitive data, or achieve Remote Code Execution (RCE).

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability. The 'name' parameter from user input is interpolated directly into HTML content without proper escaping or sanitization. An attacker can inject malicious JavaScript payloads like '<script>alert(1)</script>' that will execute in the victim's browser.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: Hardcoded sensitive credential 'SUPER_SECRET_NAME' found in source code. The secret value is hardcoded as a string literal instead of being loaded exclusively from environment variables. This exposes sensitive information in version control and increases the risk of credential leakage.

Hardcoded sensitive credential 'SUPER_SECRET_TOKEN' found in source code. The secret token value is hardcoded as a string literal instead of being loaded exclusively from environment variables. This exposes sensitive authentication tokens in version control and increases the risk of unauthorized access.

Debug mode is enabled based on configuration. When debug mode is active, FastAPI may expose detailed error messages, stack traces, and sensitive application information that could aid attackers in reconnaissance and exploitation of the application.
